rulings.Case Title: JOHN LIU et al VS. EDWARD NOVAK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Conditional Acceptance Of The Dismissal By Defts Or In The Alternative Motion For Setting A Trial Date
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 26, 2011, line 1, DEFENDANTS EDWARD NOVAK AND GLENN MENSING'S MOTION FOR CONDITIONAL ACCEPTANCE OF THE DISMISSAL BY DEFTS OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE MOTION FOR SETTING A TRIAL DATE. OFF CALENDAR, NO PROOF OF SERVICE OR COURTESY COPIES. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: FIREMAN'S FUND INSURANCE COMPANY VS. CHUBB CUSTOM INSURANACE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 26, 2011, line 2, DEFENDANT LEXINGTON INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. DEFENDANT LEXINGTON'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. AS TO THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION, THE CHUBB POLICY IMPOSES A LIMITATION ON WHEN CHUBB IS LIABLE FOR POST JUDGMENT INTEREST AND THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO WHETHER THE LIMITATION WAS MET. AS TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION, UNDER THE TERMS OF THE POLICY CHUBB WILL PAY FOR BOND AMOUNTS WITHIN "OUR LIMITS OF INSURANCE". SIMPLY BECAUSE CHUBB DEFENDED THE UNDERLYING SUIT, DOES NOT MEAN IT IS RESPONSIBLE FOR THE ENTIRE COST OF THE APPEAL WITHOUT LIMIT. WHETHER THE LIMIT WAS MET CONTINUES TO BE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: GAIL M. MCMAHON VS. PHILIP A. RICHARDSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Gail Mcmahon Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash The Amended Notice Of Deposition Of Pltf
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 26, 2011. Line 5. Plaintiff Gail Mcmahon's Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash The Amended Notice Of Deposition Of Plaintiff. Motion to quash amended notice is Granted in part and denied in part. Defendants request for documents must specify that it is for documents in plaintiffs possession, related to her specific claims or similar claims related to the subject properties. = (302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: GAIL M. MCMAHON VS. PHILIP A. RICHARDSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant The City Mews Ii Of Jennifer Place Homeowners Notice Of Motion To Compel Deposition Of Gail M Mcmahon And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 26, 2011. Line 5. Defendant The City Mews II Of Jennifer Place Homeowners' Notice Of Motion To Compel Deposition Of Gail M Mcmahon And For Sanctions. Defendant's motion to compel plaintiff's deposition is Granted. Plaintiff must arrange day(s) to be deposed (up to 10 hours) by defendant's counsel. Said deposition must take place by no later than May 20th 2011. Motion for sanctions Denied. = (301/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: GAIL M. MCMAHON VS. PHILIP A. RICHARDSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Gail Mcmahon Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Form Interrogatories To Defendant City Mews Ii Hoa And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 26, 2011. Line 5. Plaintiff Gail Mcmahon Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Plaintiff's Form Interrogatories To Defendant City Mews II And For Sanctions. Motion granted in part, denied in part. Defendant must provide further responses regarding the basis for its statute of limitations defense. Motion is Denied as to remaining items. Plaintiff cannot ask Defendant to prove negatives. Plaintiff has the burden of proof. Sanctions Denied. = (302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: JACOB BRISKMAN et al VS. TAGGED, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Protective Order From Abusive Discovery And Req. For Monetary Sanctions

PLAINTIFFS CHARLES BOHLING, BETSY BRISKMAN AND DANIELLE BRISKMAN'S Motion For Protective Order From Abusive Discovery And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Continued to 5/31/11, for the moving party to provide courtesy copies of its moving papers to department 302 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. See San Francisco Local Rule 2.6(b). = (302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: GABRIELA FLORES et al VS. PACIFIC GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Compel Answers To Deposition Questions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 26, 2011. Line 2. PLAINTIFFS GABRIELA FLORES, JAQUELINE FLORES AND SAMUEL FLORES' Notice of Motion To Compel Answers To Deposition Questions. Motion to compel answers to deposition questions is denied without prejudice. As to those questions where defendant has raised 5th. amendment objections, Plaintiff has failed to file a declaration to support the claim that the Marin Da is not investigating this matter and Defendants' declarant, Douglas Horngrad has raised possibility that defendant may incriminate himself by answering those questions as to the issue of defendants tax returns, Plaintiff has other avenues to test the employee independent contractor issue and until it has shown that these avenues have not been fruitful. Also, the empoyee defense is not tantamount to a waiver of privilege the tax issue. = (301/PJB)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Compelling Furtehr Responses To Requests For Admissions, Set One And Form Interrogatory 17.1, Set No One; And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions Against Pltf And Pltf'S Consel Michael Weiss In The Amount Of $5990

rulings.Case Title: MARY LUCAS VS. RECOMMIND, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 26, 2011, line 3, PLAINTIFF MARY LUCAS Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE A FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. WHILE PLAINTIFF MAY HAVE BEEN DILATORY IN WAITING UNTIL NOW TO REQUEST LEAVE TO AMEND, THE AMENDMENT WILL NOT REQUIRE FURTHER DISCOVERY AND TRIAL IS IN AUGUST. ACCORDINGLY, PREJUDICE TO DEFENDANT IS MINIMAL. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: LIFESTYLE DESIGN GROUP LLC, A VS. AMERICAN PEGASUS AUTO LOAN FUNDS, SP, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 26, 2011, line 8, DEFENDANT BENJAMIN CHIU'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST STATE FACTS WITH MORE PARTICULARITY AS TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION (MISREPRESENTATION). AS TO THE GROUNDS THAT DEFENDANT'S STATEMENTS ARE OPINION AND NOT ACTIONABLE, THIS IS A QUESTION OF FACT THAT IS NOT DECIDED ON A DEMURRER. DEMURRER OVERRULED ON THIS GROUND. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: HARTFORD FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY VS. WSM ENTERPRISES INC., DOING BUSINESS AS
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Joinder) To Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 26, 2011, line 10, CROSS-COMPLAINANT ONE SOURCE PLUMBING, INC. (Joinder) To Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF IS UNABLE TO SUE UNDER A RIGHT OF SUBROGATION DUE TO ITS INSURED'S INCAPICITY TO SUE. FURTHERMORE, PLAINTIFF CANNOT MAINTAIN CLAIMS FOR NEGLIGENCE AND BREACH OF IMPLIED WARRANTY AGAINST THE DEFENDANTS ON ITS OWN. =(302/LMG)
